notes: SHIP v1.14.342 - device fix pass #3 (owner re-verify of .341). Fixes 1+2 are the ship; 3+4 ride along. All redesign-scoped (#pg-cmd / body.rd) + asset re-cuts -> ?legacy byte-identical. FIX 1 (CRITICAL - hero rack != bay rack, ONE RACK ONE TRUTH): the Command hero and OPEN BAY showed the SAME rack label (s1:001) with DIFFERENT device populations (hero 6 = MSN3700/HGX H100; bay 8 incl. the real q3400-ra IB switch). Root cause = two builders off two uploads: the hero built from master_rackToElevation(racksByCab) (the loaded MASTER file's hosts) while the bay builds from deploy_loadRacks -> deployment.edpParsed.racks[].slots (the deployment EDP). Fix = cmd_resolveFirstRackElev now, WHEN an active deployment exists, resolves the deployment lead rack (deploy_loadRacks, blocked-first then rackId; D5 switch-override honored by rackId/id) and builds the elevation from it via a new thin adapter deploy_rackToElevation() -> the hero renders the SAME object the bay does (their slots already share the {uStart,uEnd,name,model,type} contract). elev.id = the DISPLAY rackId so cmd_openHeroBay's mount._rm3dRackId round-trip resolves the same deployment rack. The master_rackToElevation/racksByCab path SURVIVES ONLY as the Master-loaded-but-NO-active-deployment fallback - there OPEN BAY opens the Rack Map floor (not deploy_showRackDetail), so no hero/bay divergence is possible; the hero still lands on the first Master rack per the .330 INSPECT-3D design. FIX 2 (hero loads FLAT instead of 3D, nondeterministic; caption overlaps flat): (2a) cmd_rackHero3D no longer falls to FLAT on a layout timeout - FLAT is the WebGL-unavailable fallback ONLY per the ruling; the 120-frame->FLAT was firing when the mount hadn't laid out yet (cold boot / mid-transition) even though WebGL works. Now it retries ~600 foreground frames and, if still not laid out, LEAVES the loading state for the next cmd_render to re-arm (dedup only skips an already-live 3D rack) instead of masking with flat. cmd_mountRackHero also re-arms a bounded (~5s, 12x400ms) upgrade to 3D when reh3d_webglOK() fails TRANSIENTLY on cold iOS boot (GPU contexts not yet free) but window.WebGLRenderingContext exists - so it lands live 3D; a device with no WebGL at all stays flat, correctly. (2b) the 'RACK . id' overlay caption is hidden in FLAT (it's tuned to float over the 3D canvas and overlapped the flat elevation rows; the Z1 rackline already shows the id) and re-shown on 3D-render success. FIX 3 (BUILD nav icon sat slightly higher): re-cut all 4 nav glyph webps (phantom-nav-{home,build,tools,exit}-v2-256.webp, filenames unchanged - the CACHE_VERSION bump busts them). fix-nav.js tight-bboxed each glyph TOGETHER WITH its floor reflection; reflection length differs per icon (BUILD's blue LED spill is longest), so centering the blob left each SOLID body at a different height - BUILD's short server stack (269px vs HOME 311px) rode highest. gen-nav3.js knocks out the black bg then keeps only the LARGEST connected alpha component (discarding the detached floor mirror), then centers the clean glyph -> all four solid bodies now center within 0.5px (measured). FIX 4 (MASTER + Active pills too big): the tappable Profile chip ('Active') and the MASTER agechip were 44px tall (gloved floor) next to the ~24px Online/Ready status spans, dominating the Z0 row. Shrunk .cc-hchip-tap + .cc-agechip to the .cc-hchip visible footprint (10.5px, 6/10 pad, r14; agechip KEEPS its dark stat-card surface) and restored the 44px gloved hit via a transparent vertically-expanded ::after hit-area -> small chip, full tap target, rd-review Rule 13 (>=44px tappable) stays green. Gates: inline-JS vm.Script x3 (Fix 1/2 JS changed), CSS brace net-0 (Fix 4), sw.js ok, version.json valid, three-stamp .341->.342; phantom-ship-gate + phantom-rd-reviewer pre-commit. DEVICE-VERIFY (HARD-REFRESH / clear SW first): (1) Home hero and OPEN BAY show the SAME rack with the SAME device count/list. (2) Home hero lands in live 3D on load (not the flat list) across repeated cold loads; caption doesn't overlap. (3) the four bottom-nav icons sit on a consistent baseline (BUILD no longer high). (4) the MASTER + Active chips match the Online/Ready height yet still take a gloved tap. ?legacy=1 pixel-identical. NEXT = .343 desktop top-nav. RD-REVIEW FIX (pre-commit, P1 Rule 1): the .341->.342 hero rewrite left the SWITCH-RACK picker dead when a deployment is active - cmd_heroPickRack listed MASTER cab ids (s1:001) but the rewritten resolver matched _cmdHeroRack only against DEPLOYMENT rackIds (disjoint id space, e.g. L-01), so every pick silently fell back to the lead rack and _cmdHeroRack latched forever. Fixed BOTH sides: (a) cmd_heroPickRack now offers the SAME universe the hero renders (deployment racks when active -> keys=rackId, count=slots.length; Master cabs otherwise) and the click guard no longer requires m.racksByCab[cab] (that rejected deployment rackIds = dead tap); (b) cmd_resolveFirstRackElev honors _cmdHeroRack only on a real deployment-rack match and CLEARS a stale/foreign override (prior deployment/master swap) so the D1 lead applies - never a silent wrong-rack substitution, and the picker's current-highlight stays honest. This also keeps hero==bay for a PICKED rack (a picked deployment rack renders via deploy_rackToElevation AND cmd_openHeroBay resolves the same deployment rack). ROLLBACK: revert this commit + restore the 4 nav webps from scratchpad/nav-backup/.
